purpose: Determine whether the bounded RCCD theorem rests on a natural independently justified target class and neutral preservation contract, or on scope choices that encode RCCD into necessity.
controlling_question: Can a faithful representation of a naturally justified reasoning class avoid RCCD-equivalent commitments without losing independently specified reasoning facts?
frozen_principles: The current W9 theorem remains valid for its registered C/P/E scope unless directly refuted.
No external review or replication is authorized by this program.
The target is the strongest defensible internal answer, not accumulation of further supportive examples.
Class membership criteria must be justified independently of RCCD vocabulary.
Preservation obligations must be stated as domain-neutral reasoning distinctions and tested for construct-loading.
Adapters may not conceal primitive extensions or nonuniform recovery machinery.
Positive, plural-kernel, generic-only, bounded-negative, and unresolved outcomes remain admissible.

workstreams.title: Freeze and audit neutrality of C P and E
Execute weaker and alternative preservation contracts
Search broader effective representation families for RCCD escape
Execute frozen held-out cross-context primitive-extension tests
Issue the strongest final internal answer

downgrade_rules: If C cannot be defined independently of R1-R5, the W9 result remains formally true but is not accepted as the final universal-structure answer.
If P contains an obligation equivalent to a candidate component without independent justification, necessity for that component is classified as contract-relative and potentially construct-loaded.
If a plausible reasoning boundary case requires a genuine new primitive, RCCD universality over the naturalized class fails unless the class exclusion is independently justified.
If two faithful kernels are not bidirectionally reconstructible without hidden machinery, select a plural-kernel outcome.
If only generic transition, relation, or information-bearing structure survives all neutral contracts, select generic-only commonality.
If evidence cannot resolve naturalness, neutrality, or faithful reduction, select unresolved.
nonclaims: the W9 theorem has been externally validated
the W9 theorem is already the final answer to unrestricted universal structure
